name: SurveyMonkey API
what_it_does: Survey platform API for creating surveys, collecting responses, and analyzing results. Widely used for market research, employee feedback, customer satisfaction, and academic research.
use_cases: Creating and deploying surveys programmatically for data collection campaigns
Retrieving survey responses for analysis in agent workflows
Automating CSAT and NPS surveys triggered by business events
Building market research automation that collects and analyzes responses
Employee feedback collection and HR survey automation
not_for: Conversational/interactive forms (Typeform is better for UX)
Complex form workflows with file uploads
Real-time response processing at very low latency
best_when: An agent needs to create and manage traditional surveys at scale, especially for market research, HR feedback, or customer satisfaction measurement in enterprise contexts.
avoid_when: You need conversational forms with better UX, or are on a tight budget for high response volumes.

auth.notes: OAuth2 required. Access tokens are long-lived (no expiry by default) but can be revoked. Scopes include surveys_read, surveys_write, responses_read, etc. No simple API key option.

pricing.paid_tiers: Individual Advantage $39/month, Premier $119/month, Team plans from $25/user/month
pricing.notes: Free tier is extremely limited (40 responses max). Premium plans are expensive compared to alternatives. Team plans needed for most business use cases. API access on all plans.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include error name and message. Some errors are generic. Documentation is decent but not as developer-friendly as competitors. HTTP status codes are standard.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Survey creation not idempotent — agents must check before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 only — no API key option adds complexity for agent setup
Free tier 40-response limit per survey makes it useless for real data collection
API response data does not include question text — requires separate question lookup by ID
Bulk response retrieval is limited to 100 per page — large surveys require many requests
Survey sharing and collector management add complexity to agent deployment flows
Enterprise features (HIPAA compliance, SSO) require Enterprise plan with custom pricing
